title: Economic indicators: Key statistics for the UK economy
author: Sigma Capital
metaDescription: The UK economy in 2026 saw a rise in the S&P Global U.K. Manufacturing PMI to 53.7 in April, and GDP growth indicated by real GDP figures. Inflation and unemplo
excerpt: The UK economy in 2026 saw a rise in the S&P Global U.K. Manufacturing PMI to 53.7 in April, and GDP growth indicated by real GDP figures. Inflation and unemployment rates are key economic indicators. The latest GDP figures are essential for economic analysis.

content.introduction: The UK economy in 2026 saw a rise in the S&P Global U.K. Manufacturing PMI to 53.7 in April, and GDP growth indicated by real GDP figures. Inflation and unemployment rates are key economic indicators. The latest GDP figures are essential for economic analysis.

House prices changed at different rates across the UK over the year to December 2025. Gross Domestic Product – Real GDP: Also known as constant price or chained volume measure, this is a measure used to indicate change in the actual quantity of goods and services produced, by adjusting for inflation.
The following table shows the main economic indicators in 1980–2021 (with IMF staff estimates in 2022–2027). Inflation below 5% is in green. Year, GDP
